mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following rate cuts are not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.05) across a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating inflation, rising consumer prices force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4 percent monthly threshold, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between INDPRO momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment drop exceeding 100,000 workers signals severe labor market contraction, reducing aggregate demand and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 100,000 in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of months following a payroll decline greater than 100,000.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal produces negative forward returns in over half of observed quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply chain disruption, a sustained decline in industrial production indicates capacity constraints and rising input costs, which erode corporate margins and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of periods where INDPRO declines, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation is below 2 percent.
